title: Liquidity Stress Design

content.statement: Insolvency often happens through timing, not lack of theoretical profitability.
content.question: If your business revenue dropped 40% for 3 months, how many weeks could you operate before cutting?
content.equation: LSD = (Cash Buffer + Credit + Conversion Speed + Variable Cost Flex) ÷ Fixed Burn
sop: Calculate your current Cash Buffer in months of Fixed Burn covered (see calculation, page 5).
List available Credit (lines of credit, factoring, owner capital) as additional months of runway (see calculation, page 5).
Rate your Conversion Speed and Variable Cost Flex 1-10, how fast you collect cash and how much of your spend you could cut on short notice (see calculation, page 5).
Run a written 40% revenue drop for 3 months scenario and calculate how many weeks you'd survive.
If the answer is under 8 weeks, build a specific cash reserve or credit-line target this quarter.
content.explainer: Profitable companies die from cash timing mismatches. Model the stress before it arrives.
questions: If your business revenue dropped 40% for 3 months, how many weeks could you operate before cutting?
How much of your monthly costs are truly fixed versus costs you could cut within two weeks if revenue fell?
Do you have a pre-arranged credit line or backup cash source, or would a bad quarter mean scrambling for a loan?
content.equation_example: Cash Buffer 2 months, Credit 1 month, Conversion Speed rated 7 out of 10, Variable Cost Flex rated 6 out of 10, divided by Fixed Burn rated 8 out of 10. (2 plus 1 plus 7 plus 6) divided by 8 equals 2.0. Higher scores mean you can absorb a revenue shock for longer before cutting; lower scores mean a bad quarter could force emergency decisions fast.
content.closing_statement: Cash timing kills faster than bad unit economics.

caption:
Principle Nº 50: Liquidity Stress Design

Insolvency often happens through timing, not lack of theoretical profitability. Profitable companies die from cash timing mismatches.

Ask yourself: If your business revenue dropped 40% for 3 months, how many weeks could you operate before cutting?
